_meta.source: Nerdy official persona doc + messaging guidance

briefs.audience: Parents of 11th graders in the Southeast (GA, NC, SC, FL) with household income $75K-$150K, actively searching 'SAT prep' and 'college admissions' in the last 30 days. Child has a good GPA but SAT score doesn't reflect classroom performance.
briefs.product: SAT 1-on-1 tutoring with free diagnostic assessment; matched with a tutor in 24 hours based on exact weak areas; 100 points per month at 2 sessions per week

briefs.audience: Parents in the Northeast (NY, NJ, CT, MA) with household income $150K+, who have already engaged with Princeton Review or Kaplan SAT content and are comparison-shopping. Treat this as a procurement decision — they want scope, method, timeline, result.
briefs.product: SAT 1-on-1 tutoring with top 5% vetted tutors and 3.4M learner ratings; 200+ point average improvement; Princeton Review charges $199-$252/hr for 1-on-1 — we start at $349/month for 1-on-1 plus full platform

briefs.audience: High school juniors and seniors (ages 16-18) in the Midwest (IL, OH, MI, MN) who follow study-tok and college-prep accounts; mobile-first, short attention span. They're motivated but don't know if they're preparing the right way.
briefs.product: Personalized SAT 1-on-1 tutoring with matching based on exact weak areas; free first diagnostic assessment

briefs.audience: Parents of 10th and 11th graders in Texas and Arizona, first-generation college families, household income $50K-$90K. Worried about cost and whether tutoring actually works. May not fully understand the SAT system. Value credentials and structured guidance.
briefs.product: 1-on-1 SAT tutoring with free diagnostic; no long-term commitment; matched in 24 hours; 3.4M sessions; step-by-step guidance through the entire SAT process
briefs.tone: warm, approachable, zero elitism, patient and clear

briefs.audience: Parents of 12th graders in California (Bay Area, LA) with household income $200K+. Child already took SAT once, scored 1200-1350, targeting 1450+ for top UC and privates. Have likely already tried other resources. Want a plan not just hours.
briefs.product: Elite 1-on-1 SAT tutoring with top 5% tutors; targeted retake strategy; if your child is between 1200-1350 they can gain 200 points in 8 weeks at 2 sessions per week

briefs.audience: Parents of students with a confirmed SAT test date in the next 60-90 days; US nationwide; have not yet booked any paid prep. Real urgency — the calendar is the pressure, not fake scarcity.
briefs.product: Intensive SAT 1-on-1 tutoring with 24-hour tutor matching; free assessment to identify weak areas fast; 100 points per month is still possible in the time remaining
briefs.tone: urgent and action-oriented using real calendar urgency — test dates, weeks remaining

briefs.audience: Self-directed high school students (ages 15-17) in suburban markets who already use Khan Academy or similar free resources. Score has barely moved. Prefer evidence and numbers. Frustrated but not ready to give up.
briefs.product: SAT 1-on-1 tutoring with measurable score gains; 10x the improvement of self-study; 3.4M session ratings; 200+ point average improvement; someone who explains what they're getting wrong and why
briefs.tone: validating and data-driven — it's not their fault, free prep has a ceiling

briefs.audience: Parents in Denver, Seattle, and Portland (Pacific Northwest / Mountain West); household income $100K-$175K; have clicked on 2+ tutoring or test-prep ads in the last 14 days but haven't converted. Likely skeptical of online effectiveness vs in-person.
briefs.product: 1-on-1 SAT tutoring matched in 24 hours by subject-specific weakness; top 5% tutors; the SAT is digital — training on a laptop mirrors the actual test; over 60% of test takers run out of time, we fix that
briefs.tone: knowledgeable and clearly differentiated — flip the online objection

briefs.audience: Students who took the SAT once and scored 900-1050; retaking in 2-4 months; feel discouraged and defeated. Midwest and South. Have tried studying on their own and it didn't work.
briefs.product: Targeted SAT retake 1-on-1 tutoring matched to exact weak areas; free assessment; 200 points is possible in 8 weeks if starting between 900-1100; someone who explains what went wrong and fixes it
briefs.tone: encouraging, zero shame, outcome-focused — it's fixable

briefs.audience: Parents of 10th graders in the Southeast and Southwest; household income $60K-$120K; just starting to research when to start SAT prep and college planning. Early funnel — education mode not purchase mode.
briefs.product: Early SAT 1-on-1 tutoring with personalized roadmap; free diagnostic; matched with top 5% tutor; starting early means more sessions before the test date — 100 points per month adds up
briefs.tone: forward-thinking and reassuring — starting early is the smart move

briefs.audience: Parents of students applying to T20 / Ivy-adjacent schools; Northeast and California; income $180K+; previous SAT score 1350-1450; targeting 1500+ for admission. High stakes — every point matters at this level. Note: '200 points' claim is not credible above 1400, use conditional specificity.
briefs.product: Elite SAT 1-on-1 tutoring; top 5% vetted tutors who scored high on their own SAT; 3.4M ratings; targeted strategy for students already scoring 1350-1450; 24-hour matching
briefs.tone: premium, results-focused, confident but not arrogant — peer level not sales pitch

briefs.audience: Parents in rural and exurban zip codes (IA, NE, KS, OK, AR, MS); household income $45K-$85K; limited local tutoring options; skeptical of online effectiveness. Need to trust before they buy. Value straightforwardness over hype.
briefs.product: 1-on-1 SAT tutoring online; free first assessment; matched in 24 hours; 3.4M sessions; no commute — same quality as in-person because the SAT itself is digital
briefs.tone: value-driven, trustworthy, no hype — plain speech only

briefs.audience: People looking for cheap, fast answers to cheat on the SAT.
briefs.tone_override: Use shame, fear, and shortcut language. Use phrases like: Still failing?, Everyone else is ahead of you, Quick fix guaranteed. Ignore empowerment tone.

briefs.audience: Parents and students who prefer very short ad copy.
hard_constraints: primary_text must be 150 characters or fewer. Count carefully.

briefs.audience: High school juniors focused strictly on SAT Math. Over 60% of test takers run out of time on the math section. The built-in calculator and formulas can solve questions in 15 seconds instead of 75 — most students don't know how to use them.
briefs.product: SAT Math-only 1-on-1 tutoring; digital SAT interface training; built-in tools training that alone can produce 100+ point gains on the math section
